Question: Understanding Life and Physics - Charles Darwin's theory of natural selection states that organisms that are better adapted to their environment are more likely to:
B: B. Move to a different environment
C: C. Survive and pass on their traits to offspring
Answer: C. Survive and pass on their traits to offspring

Subject: Science
Question: Understanding Life and Physics - The Aeta people of the Philippines developed darker skin and shorter stature over generations living in dense forests. This is an example of:
A: A. Genetic mutation caused by forest chemicals
B: B. Co-dominance between two allele types
C: C. Adaptation through natural selection over generations
Answer: C. Adaptation through natural selection over generations

Question: Verbals & Modifiers - "The scholar having reviewed all the material felt confident going into the exam." Which of the following correctly identifies the underlined verbal phrase?
A: A. Participial phrase acting as an adjective modifying "scholar"
B: B. Infinitive phrase acting as an adverb modifying "felt"
C: C. Gerund phrase acting as the object of the sentence
Answer: A. Participial phrase acting as an adjective modifying "scholar"

Subject: Science
Question: Speed & Evolution - A species of fox living in the Arctic has thick white fur. A few foxes are born with thinner brown fur. What will most likely happen to the brown foxes over time?
A: A. The brown foxes will gradually grow thicker white fur to survive
B: B. The brown foxes will struggle to survive and fewer will pass on their traits
C: C. The brown foxes will thrive because they stand out and attract mates
Answer: B. The brown foxes will struggle to survive and fewer will pass on their traits

Subject: Science
Question: Speed & Evolution - Which of the following best explains why evolution takes millions of years?
A: A. Organisms resist change and only evolve when forced to by predators
B: B. Scientists have not yet found a way to speed up the process
C: C. Small genetic changes accumulate slowly across thousands of generations before significant differences appear
Answer: C. Small genetic changes accumulate slowly across thousands of generations before significant differences appear
